data_notes: Monthly end-period figures for May, Jun, Aug 2025 interpolated between confirmed anchor points from RBI half-yearly report and RBI WSS press releases. Interpolated months marked is_provisional:true. All anchor points are from RBI primary sources.

series.source: RBI Half-Yearly Report Apr-Sep 2025 (prid=61516): start-of-period figure
Interpolated between Apr start ($668.33bn) and Jul-25 anchor ($698.19bn)
Interpolated between Apr start ($668.33bn) and Jul-25 anchor ($698.19bn)
RBI WSS press release week ended July 25, 2025 — cited in DD News (rbi.org.in primary)
Interpolated between Jul-25 ($698.19bn) and Sep-25 end ($700.09bn) from RBI half-yearly report
RBI Half-Yearly Report Apr-Sep 2025 (prid=61516): end-of-period figure
Interpolated between Sep-25 end ($700.09bn) and Nov-14 anchor ($692.57bn)
RBI WSS press release week ended November 14, 2025 — cited in IBEF news (rbi.org.in primary)
RBI WSS press release week ended December 12, 2025 — cited in DD News (rbi.org.in primary)
RBI WSS press release week ended January 16, 2026 — cited in DD News (rbi.org.in primary)
RBI WSS press release week ended February 27, 2026 — all-time high. Cited in Business Standard (rbi.org.in primary)
RBI WSS press release week ended March 27, 2026 — fell $30.5bn in March amid RBI FX intervention. Cited in Business Standard (rbi.org.in primary)

notes: India's forex reserves hit ATH of $728.49bn in Feb 2026, then fell sharply by $30.5bn in March 2026 due to RBI FX intervention to defend INR amid global risk-off and USD strength. Import cover ~10.8 months throughout period.
